name: GANGADHAR KURMA
skills: Machine Learning & Deep Learning (CNN, VAE, RNN)

responsibilities: Designed and deployed data analytics pipelines that extracted insights from large-scale CSV and Excel files, enabling business teams to automate reporting and decision-making.
Built a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) application for a specific client, integrating external vector databases to improve accuracy and domain-specific knowledge retrieval.
Developed a Chat-with-Database application that translated natural language queries into SQL, allowing non-technical users to interact seamlessly with structured data.
Engineered a ChatGPT-like replica for internal use, supporting summarization, Q&A, and knowledge-base assistance for enterprise workflows.
multiple production-grade LLM modules with fine-tuning and optimized API/middleware integration, improving scalability and response quality.
Delivered seamless model integration and engineered over 15 high-quality prompts for LLM-based solutions, improving response relevance by 30%.
Optimized middleware and deployment scripts, reducing model response latency by 30% and improving output accuracy across 3+ production LLM modules
Delivered 5 production-grade features leveraging OpenAI models for legal document summarization, intelligent search, and workflow automation.
Designed and optimized prompt-based automations that streamlined legal operations and improved user assistance efficiency.
Contributed to middleware development and API integration, ensuring seamless interaction between LLM services and enterprise applications.
Improved accuracy, scalability, and interpretability of LLM outputs through strategic task segmentation and fine-tuning approaches.

experience.company: IDRBT – Institute for Development & Research in Banking Technology
responsibilities: Fraud Detection: Developed a VAE-based anomaly detection model using chaos theory, achieving 92% precision in detecting fraud across healthcare and insurance datasets.
Association Rule Mining: Scaled FP-Growth and Apriori algorithms with PySpark on YARN clusters to uncover transaction patterns and improve risk analysis in banking.
Explainable AI (XAI): Applied LIME and SHAP to churn and NPA prediction models, improving model transparency, interpretability, and regulatory compliance.
Sentiment Analysis: Built a scalable ML pipeline with PySpark, Docker, and MLflow to analyze large volumes of banking app reviews, providing actionable customer insights.
Churn Prediction POC (MLOps): Conducted a proof-of-concept on bank customer data, designing, training, and deploying end-to-end churn prediction models using MLOps best practices.

AI Knowledge Transfer: Conducted training sessions and workshops for bankers, explaining how AI/ML techniques like anomaly detection, XAI, sentiment analysis, and churn prediction could be applied to their day-to-day use cases.

education.institution: AIMS College of Engineering – JNTU Kakinada
